tossups.question: The façade of one of these buildings features a sculpture that was attacked with a bottle of ink for its nude figures. After the section of the Ringstrasse in front of one of these buildings was raised by a meter, residents of Vienna nicknamed it the “sunken treasure chest.” Profits from the rubber boom funded the construction of a building of this type named for the Amazon in Manaus. Colorful ceiling painting by (*) Marc Chagall surround a massive chandelier in one of these places, where Isidore Pils’s sculpture The Triumph of Apollo and several onyx balconies look down upon a white marble “Grand Staircase.” The Palais Garnier is one of these buildings. For 10 points, name this sort of building exemplified by a “shelled” one designed by Jørn Utzon on Sydney Harbor.
tossups.answer: opera houses [accept Sydney Opera House; accept Paris Opera or Opéra de Paris; accept Vienna State Opera or Wiener Staatsoper; prompt on theaters or performing arts centers or auditoriums or concert halls by asking “what specific performances are the theaters intended for?”; prompt on palaces or palais]
tossups.answer_sanitized: opera houses [accept Sydney Opera House; accept Paris Opera or Opera de Paris; accept Vienna State Opera or Wiener Staatsoper; prompt on theaters or performing arts centers or auditoriums or concert halls by asking "what specific performances are the theaters intended for?"; prompt on palaces or palais]
tossups.question_sanitized: The facade of one of these buildings features a sculpture that was attacked with a bottle of ink for its nude figures. After the section of the Ringstrasse in front of one of these buildings was raised by a meter, residents of Vienna nicknamed it the "sunken treasure chest." Profits from the rubber boom funded the construction of a building of this type named for the Amazon in Manaus. Colorful ceiling painting by (*) Marc Chagall surround a massive chandelier in one of these places, where Isidore Pils's sculpture The Triumph of Apollo and several onyx balconies look down upon a white marble "Grand Staircase." The Palais Garnier is one of these buildings. For 10 points, name this sort of building exemplified by a "shelled" one designed by Jorn Utzon on Sydney Harbor.

tossups.question: The Dennis Wolanski library and archives are located in this building. Immediately before this building was completed, its architect used a similar aesthetic for the design of the Kuwait National Assembly. The Forecort of this building was recently closed down to add a new tunnel, and contains a series of large steps that can be used as seating. One room in this building contains a blotchy 40-foot woolen tapestry and was renamed for the (*) architect after the government resolved their dispute with him. This Ove Arup-engineered building is located on Bannelong point and includes a playhouse, drama theatre, and concert hall. For 10 points, name this building initially designed by Jorn Utzon with several white curved shells on its roof, a musical venue in Australia.
tossups.question_sanitized: The Dennis Wolanski library and archives are located in this building. Immediately before this building was completed, its architect used a similar aesthetic for the design of the Kuwait National Assembly. The Forecort of this building was recently closed down to add a new tunnel, and contains a series of large steps that can be used as seating. One room in this building contains a blotchy 40-foot woolen tapestry and was renamed for the (*) architect after the government resolved their dispute with him. This Ove Arup-engineered building is located on Bannelong point and includes a playhouse, drama theatre, and concert hall. For 10 points, name this building initially designed by Jorn Utzon with several white curved shells on its roof, a musical venue in Australia.

bonuses.leadin: An architect sought to blend the designs of a tower and podium for a building in this city. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this city where the Innovation Tower was built for its Polytechnic University. A distinctive I. M. Pei skyscraper in this city received criticism for the “X” shapes formed by its exterior of adjacent white triangles.
This architect, the “queen of the curve,” designed the Innovation Tower. The London Aquatics Center for the 2012 Olympics and Rome’s MAXXI museum were also designed by this British-Iraqi architect.

Hadid used two rocks being washed away by the Pearl River as the inspiration for a building of this type in Guangzhou. Sails inspired the iconic white roof of one of these buildings in Sydney designed by Jørn Utzon.
answers: Hong Kong (The Pei building is the Bank of China Tower.)
Zaha Hadid [or Dame Zaha Mohammad Hadid DBE RA]
opera house [or the Guangzhou Opera House; or the Sydney Opera House]
answers_sanitized: Hong Kong (The Pei building is the Bank of China Tower.)
Zaha Hadid [or Dame Zaha Mohammad Hadid DBE RA]
opera house [or the Guangzhou Opera House; or the Sydney Opera House]

bonuses.leadin: Eero Saarinen famously pulled the design for this building out of a pile of rejected submissions, and essentially forced the other judges to use it. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this building that sits on Bennelong Point and looks like it is covered in sails. At its 1973 opening, the architect was neither invited nor mentioned.
The winning design for the Sydney Opera House was submitted by this architect that practiced "additive architecture" and designed the National Assembly Building in Kuwait.
In 1982, Jørn Utzon received an award from the Finnish Association of Architects named for this designer of the Essen Opera House and the Finlandia Hall.
